name: Sunbelt Pawn Jewelry & Loan #17
description_short: Family-owned pawn shop chain operating 14+ locations across Houston, Dallas, Irving, and Fort Worth since 2006, offering collateral-based loans, merchandise sales, and jewelry repair services.
description_long:
Sunbelt Pawn is a family-owned and locally operated pawn shop business established in 2006, starting from a single location on 34th Street and Ella Boulevard in Houston, Texas. The company has expanded to operate over 14 pawn stores serving the Houston, Irving, and Dallas/Fort Worth metropolitan areas, positioning itself as a community-focused alternative financial service provider.

The company offers a range of services centered on collateral-based lending and merchandise retail. Their core offerings include pawn loans (short-term cash advances secured by personal items), jewelry repair services, FFL (Federal Firearms License) transfers, and the sale of used merchandise including luxury handbags, fine jewelry, firearms, musical instruments, audio equipment, electronics, and power tools. They also maintain online shopping options through their website and eBay marketplace.

Sunbelt Pawn emphasizes respectful customer treatment and confidentiality as distinguishing factors, marketing themselves as having "helped change the face of the once-mysterious business" of pawn lending. They highlight professional and experienced staff, and their expansion to 14+ locations suggests operational stability and community acceptance. The company also demonstrates community investment through scholarship programs and career opportunities.

As a pawn shop, Sunbelt Pawn serves consumers needing immediate cash without credit checks, but the service inherently involves surrendering personal property as collateral. The limited website information does not disclose specific loan terms, interest rates, or redemption periods, making it difficult for consumers to assess true costs before visiting a location. Their strength lies in accessibility and speed of funding rather than borrower protection or transparency.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: 19 convenient locations across Houston, Dallas/Fort Worth area for accessibility
Family-owned and locally operated since 2006 with demonstrated stability
No credit check required for pawn loans—accessible to consumers with poor credit
Immediate cash funding without lengthy application processes
Diverse merchandise selection including luxury items, jewelry, firearms, and electronics
Additional services beyond pawning: jewelry repair and FFL transfers
Strong customer reviews (5.0 rating shown for location #1 with 2,468 reviews)
cons: Website does not disclose specific loan terms, interest rates, or redemption timeframes
Requires surrendering personal property as collateral—risk of loss if unable to repay
Limited transparency about fees, loan duration, or renewal terms on main website
Pawn loans typically involve higher effective interest rates compared to traditional lending
No information about loan qualification criteria or maximum/minimum loan amounts
best_for: Consumers needing immediate cash without credit checks or lengthy approval processes
Individuals with poor credit history or thin credit files unable to qualify for traditional loans
People with valuable items (jewelry, electronics, instruments) willing to use them as collateral for short-term cash needs
diagnosis: Sunbelt Pawn is best suited for individuals facing urgent cash needs who own valuable collateral and either lack access to traditional credit or prefer to avoid credit checks. The main caveat is that pawn loans involve surrendering personal property and typically carry high effective interest rates; borrowers should only pawn items they can afford to lose and should carefully understand redemption terms (which are not disclosed on the website) before proceeding.
services: Merchandise retail (luxury handbags, fine jewelry, firearms, musical instruments)
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 3733 E Belknap St, Fort Worth, TX 76111
